title: plantago maritima subsp. subpolaris
intro: Plantago maritima subsp. subpolaris, also known as sea plantain, is a subspecies of the plantain family that grows in arctic and subarctic regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and flavonoids. The health benefits of Plantago maritima subsp. subpolaris include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
